vault.title: What is thirdweb Vault?
vault.description: thirdweb Vault is a secure key management system that allows your workflows to create and control blockchain wallets for automated operations - like a robot that can sign transactions on your behalf.

features.title: Create Wallets in Workflows
features.description: Your automation workflows can create dedicated blockchain wallets for specific tasks (trading bots, NFT minting, etc.)

features.title: Sign Transactions Automatically
features.description: Workflows can sign blockchain transactions, messages, and smart contract calls without manual approval

features.title: Multi-Chain Support
features.description: Works across all 35+ supported blockchain networks - Ethereum, Polygon, Base, Arbitrum, and more

features.title: You Stay in Control
features.description: Only you can access and manage your workflow wallets - they're tied to your account

nitro.title: AWS Nitro Enclaves
nitro.description: Private keys are stored in AWS Nitro Enclaves - isolated, hardware-protected environments that even AWS administrators cannot access.

encryption.title: End-to-End Encryption
encryption.description: All communication with vault wallets uses xChaCha20-Poly1305 encryption with perfect forward secrecy.
features: Private keys never leave the enclave
Encrypted at rest and in transit

hero.title: Building the Future of
hero.description: FrostyLabs is on a mission to democratize AI automation and make blockchain technology accessible to everyone. We're building the tools that will power the next generation of automated workflows.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.paragraph1: We believe that AI automation should be accessible, secure, and truly decentralized. Our mission is to democratize AI workflow automation by combining the power of multiple AI models with the security and ownership benefits of blockchain technology.
mission.paragraph2: With simple USDC subscriptions and open agent protocols like x402 and ERC-8004, we're building a platform where anyone can create, deploy, and monetize on-chain agents — and capture the value they generate in the ecosystem.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.description: A world where AI automation is as accessible and tradable as any digital asset, where users control their own destiny, and where the power of artificial intelligence is harnessed by everyone, not just large corporations.

techStack.title: Technology Stack
techStack.subtitle: Built with cutting-edge technologies for maximum performance, security, and scalability.

roadmap.title: Roadmap
roadmap.subtitle: Our journey to revolutionize AI automation and blockchain integration.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about FrostyFi

items.question: What is FrostyFi?
items.answer: FrostyFi is a visual platform for building AI agents that act on-chain. Connect a large language model to blockchain operations, HTTP calls, or conditional logic using a drag-and-drop canvas — no smart-contract expertise required. Deploy your agent, expose it via a standard API, and let it run autonomously.

items.question: Do I need to write code?
items.answer: No. FrostyFi's no-code visual builder lets you wire together AI models, on-chain actions, and external APIs by dragging nodes onto a canvas. Power users can drop in custom JavaScript nodes for anything the built-in library doesn't cover.

items.question: Which chains and AI models are supported?
items.answer: On the blockchain side, FrostyFi supports EVM-compatible networks (Ethereum, Base, Polygon, Arbitrum, Optimism, and more) as well as Solana. For AI, you can route requests through OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Groq, and additional providers via OpenRouter — swap models per node without rebuilding your workflow.

items.question: How do payments work?
items.answer: Two layers: the app itself runs on simple USDC subscriptions on Base (Pro and Enterprise tiers) — pay in stablecoin, no NFT and no token required. Agent-to-agent payments use x402 — a per-request micropayment standard that settles in USDC on Base with no intermediary. Your agents can charge callers and pay other agents without any manual invoicing.

items.question: What is x402?
items.answer: x402 is an open standard that uses the HTTP 402 "Payment Required" response to let a client pay for a request and instantly retry — no accounts, API keys or subscriptions. On FrostyFi, your agents can charge per call in USDC on Base and pay other agents the same way, so an API or agent gets paid the moment it's used.

items.question: What is ERC-8004?
items.answer: ERC-8004 is an Ethereum standard for "trustless agents": it gives every agent a portable on-chain identity and reputation through public registries, so counterparties can verify who they're paying and trusting. FrostyFi can register your agent on the ERC-8004 registry in one click, so it carries a verifiable identity that lives on-chain.

items.question: Is there a Permafrost ($PERMA) token?
items.answer: The Permafrost ($PERMA) token is planned for 2026. It is not live today. When it launches, Permafrost is designed to serve as the platform's utility and governance token — locking, fee discounts, and protocol-owned liquidity. Current subscriptions are simple USDC payments on Base — no NFT and no token purchase is needed to use FrostyFi now.

items.question: How do I get started?
items.answer: Sign up at flow.frostylabs.ai. The free tier gives you access to the visual builder and a set of AI calls per month to explore the platform. No wallet setup is required to register — connect a wallet later when you want to use on-chain features.
